gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Handle an [`Outcome`] under a recovery policy, providing the error's class for rule lookup."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `class_of` extracts the error's class for policy lookup (never an eval'd string — X1). It"] # [doc = " returns an **owned** [`ClassName`] (resolved through the registry at policy-build time; the"] # [doc = " returned name is already validated — X1)."] # [doc = " `attempt` is called at most `max_attempts` times for a `Retry` action, yielding a new"] # [doc = " `(Outcome, GuaranteeStrength)` pair (the strength is the attempt value's honest tag)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Never-silent (I1 / C1)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every call yields `Recovered | Propagated`; no path drops an error.  The [`Resolution`] type"] # [doc = " has no `Dropped` variant — I1 is enforced by the type."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tags (I2 / FR-R3 / VR-5)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Ok(v)` → `Recovered(v, Exact, None)` — the honest tag for a clean pass-through."] # [doc = " - `Fallback` → `Recovered(v, Declared, policy_ref)` — honest floor for a substitution."] # [doc = " - `Retry` success → `Recovered(v, attempt_tag, policy_ref)` — inherits the attempt's tag."] # [doc = " - `Retry` exhausted → `Propagated(original_error, policy_ref, cleanup_overrun: false)`."] # [doc = " - `Escalate` → `Propagated(error, policy_ref, cleanup_overrun: false)`."] # [doc = " - `CleanupThenPropagate` → `Propagated(error, policy_ref, cleanup_overrun: <bool>)`."] # [doc = " - No matching rule → `Propagated(error, None, cleanup_overrun: false)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects (C6 / I3 / I4)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Retry` consumes `EffectKind::Retry` once per attempt from `budgets`. Budget overrun →"] # [doc = "   immediate `Propagated(original_error)`."] # [doc = " - `CleanupThenPropagate` consumes the action's declared `effect` once. A budget overrun sets"] # [doc = "   `cleanup_overrun: true` in the `Propagated` outcome (spec §7-Q4 disposition: record, not"] # [doc = "   swallow)."] # [doc = " - `Fallback` and `Escalate` are effect-free."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Policy identity (banked guard #5)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " When a rule matches, the acting [`PolicyRef`](crate::policy::PolicyRef) is computed via"] # [doc = " [`RecoveryPolicy::policy_ref`] (a stable serde_json encoding — not `Debug`).  If the fallback"] # [doc = " value's `serde::Serialize` impl fails (e.g. a `NaN` float), the function propagates the"] # [doc = " original error with `policy: None` — it refuses to apply the action without an established"] # [doc = " policy identity (never-silent: the error is never dropped, I1).  This situation is a"] # [doc = " configuration error in the caller's `T: Serialize` impl; the explicit `Propagated` makes it"] # [doc = " diagnosable."] # [must_use] pub fn handle_classified < T , E > (outcome : Outcome < T , E > , policy : & RecoveryPolicy < T > , budgets : & mut Budgets , class_of : impl Fn (& E) -> ClassName , mut attempt : impl FnMut () -> (Outcome < T , E > , GuaranteeStrength) ,) -> Resolution < T , E > where T : Serialize + std :: fmt :: Debug + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static , { let error = match outcome { Outcome :: Ok (value) => { return Resolution :: Recovered { value , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , policy : None , } ; } Outcome :: Err (e) => e , } ; let class = class_of (& error) ; let Some (action) = policy . action_for (& class) else { return Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : None , cleanup_overrun : false , } ; } ; let pref = match policy . policy_ref () { Ok (r) => Some (r) , Err (_) => { return Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : None , cleanup_overrun : false , } ; } } ; match action { RecoveryAction :: Fallback { value } => { Resolution :: Recovered { value : * value . clone () , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , policy : pref , } } RecoveryAction :: Retry { max_attempts } => { let max = * max_attempts ; for _ in 0 .. max { if budgets . consume (mycelium_interp :: budget :: EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . is_err () { return Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , cleanup_overrun : false , } ; } let (next_outcome , attempt_tag) = attempt () ; if let Outcome :: Ok (value) = next_outcome { return Resolution :: Recovered { value , tag : attempt_tag , policy : pref , } ; } } Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , cleanup_overrun : false , } } RecoveryAction :: Escalate { to_class } => { let _ = to_class ; Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , cleanup_overrun : false , } } RecoveryAction :: CleanupThenPropagate { effect } => { let cleanup_overrun = budgets . consume (effect . clone () , 1) . is_err () ; Resolution :: Propagated { error , policy : pref , cleanup_overrun , } } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Convenience: bridge a `Result<T, E>` into a [`Resolution<T, E>`] under a policy."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is [`handle_classified`] with a `Result` input.  `RecoverOutcome<T, E>` is"] # [doc = " `Resolution<T, E>` — the concrete shape that resolves `error.md` §7-Q1 (no drop variant, I1;"] # [doc = " honest inherited tag, I2)."] # [must_use] pub fn recover_classified < T , E > (result : Result < T , E > , policy : & RecoveryPolicy < T > , budgets : & mut Budgets , class_of : impl Fn (& E) -> ClassName , attempt : impl FnMut () -> (Outcome < T , E > , GuaranteeStrength) ,) -> Resolution < T , E > where T : Serialize + std :: fmt :: Debug + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static , { handle_classified (Outcome :: from_result (result) , policy , budgets , class_of , attempt ,) }
